title: Festive Bitter Middle Eastern Chickpeas Hummus Fondue
description: A unique and refreshing dessert featuring bitter cocoa powder, pillowy chickpeas, and Middle Eastern flavors.

ingredients.name: Pita chips or vegetables for serving

instructions: Drain and rinse the chickpeas, and transfer them to a blender or food processor.
Add the lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, cumin, cocoa powder, salt, and black pepper to the blender.
Blend the mixture on high speed until smooth and creamy, stopping to scrape down the sides of the blender as needed.
With the blender running, slowly add the warm water and continue blending until the hummus reaches the desired consistency.
Transfer the hummus to a fondue pot or a small saucepan and stir in the chopped parsley.
Serve the hummus warm, accompanied by pita chips or vegetables for dipping.
If the hummus becomes too thick, you can thin it out with a little more warm water.
If the hummus becomes too thin, you can thicken it by adding a little more chickpeas or tahini.
You can also adjust the amount of garlic or cumin to taste.
For a more intense flavor, you can add a pinch of cayenne pepper or red pepper flakes.
For a thicker hummus, you can add a little more tahini or sesame paste.
For a creamier hummus, you can add a little more olive oil or lemon juice.
